2605.21963.title: ChronoMedicalWorld: A Medical World Model for Learning Patient Trajectories from Longitudinal Care Data
2605.21963.paper_summary: Long-horizon clinical simulation -- predicting how a patient's physiology evolves over years under specified interventions -- is central to chronic-disease care, yet existing electronic health record (EHR) models are predominantly discriminative, and general-purpose large language models drift under repeated interventions. We propose the \textbf{ChronoMedicalWorld Model (CMWM)}, an action-conditioned latent world-model framework for learning patient trajectories from longitudinal care data. CMWM couples a joint-embedding state encoder with a wide action encoder that admits both structured intervention indicators and free-text communication embeddings, and trains a recurrent latent transition module under a six-term objective: next-observation supervision, next-latent prediction, SIGReg latent regularisation, and three physiology-aware shape priors (slope, continuity, large-jump penalty). A closed-loop rollout-prefix protocol matches training to deployment, so the model is optimised against the same multi-step error it exhibits at inference. As a concrete case study, we instantiate CMWM for annual estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) trajectory forecasting in chronic kidney disease (CKD). On a 2{,}232-patient nephrology cohort, the CKD instantiation achieves a dynamic-50\% history rollout test mean absolute error (MAE) of 7.384 and root-mean-square error (RMSE) of 10.256, against 7.964 and 11.069 for a tuned GPT-5.5 structured-prompting baseline ($-7.28\%$ MAE, $-7.35\%$ RMSE), with the gain dominated by the dialogue portion of patient--health-coach communication. The framework is not CKD-specific: its architecture, loss design, and training protocol apply to any chronic condition that can be cast as periodic clinical state interleaved with structured and conversational interventions.
2605.21963.paper_summary_zh: 長期臨床模擬——預測患者生理在特定干預下如何隨著年份演變——對於慢性病護理至關重要，然而現有的電子健康記錄（EHR）模型主要是判別性的，且通用的大型語言模型在重複干預下會發生漂移。我們提出了\textbf{ChronoMedicalWorld Model (CMWM)}，這是一個基於行動條件的潛在世界模型框架，用於從長期護理數據中學習患者軌跡。CMWM將聯合嵌入狀態編碼器與寬行動編碼器相結合，該編碼器接受結構化干預指標和自由文本通信嵌入，並在六項目標下訓練一個遞歸潛在轉換模塊：下一觀察監督、下一潛在預測、SIGReg潛在正則化，以及三個生理感知形狀先驗（斜率、連續性、大跳懲罰）。閉環回饋前綴協議將訓練與部署相匹配，因此模型針對其在推理時表現出的相同多步誤差進行優化。作為具體案例研究，我們為慢性腎病（CKD）的年度估計腎小管過濾率（eGFR）軌跡預測實現了CMWM。在一個2,232名患者的腎科隊列中，CKD實現的動態50\%歷史回饋測試平均絕對誤差（MAE）為7.384，均方根誤差（RMSE）為10.256，而調整後的GPT-5.5結構提示基準分別為7.964和11.069（MAE降低7.28\%，RMSE降低7.35\%），增益主要來自患者與健康教練之間的對話部分。該框架並不特定於CKD：其架構、損失設計和訓練協議適用於任何可以被視為定期臨床狀態與結構化和對話干預交織的慢性病。
2605.21963.author: Jiangyuan Wang et.al.
2605.21963.authors: Jiangyuan Wang, Xuyong Chen, Junwei He, Xu Xu, Shasha Xie, Fuman Han
